title: monitoring — TenancyHook + lifecycle hooks
description: Cross-module hook implementations. TenancyHooks are the tenancy module's contract for reacting to tenant-lifecycle events (provision / suspend / erase). Monitoring registers three hooks: tenant erasure cascade, tenant-suspend provider deactivation, tenant-resume re-activation. No bootstrapper — monitoring does not need per-request tenant hydration.

hooks.name: PurgeMonitoringDataForErasedTenant
hooks.purpose: Cascade delete via FK CASCADE across the six monitoring tables. This hook writes the compliance audit trail entry + emits count metrics per entity family (health_checks / health_check_runs / monitoring_alert_policies / monitoring_alerts / monitoring_incidents / monitoring_provider_configs purged). No cross-provider deletion requests — monitoring providers store system-health events, not user data; the tenant's own identifier is the only correlation surface and the tenant is being erased.
hooks.compliance: GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure). Emitted metrics feed the compliance dashboard for retention proof. Since monitoring rows carry no user PII beyond tenant_id, the erasure is a full FK CASCADE — no partial redaction path needed.

hooks.name: SuspendMonitoringProvidersOnTenantSuspended
hooks.purpose: Marks every monitoring_provider_config for the suspended tenant as is_active=false + disables every health_check (is_enabled=false + cancels pending RunHealthCheckJob). Does NOT soft-delete — restoration is one flip away via ResumeMonitoringProvidersOnTenantResumed. Fires MonitoringProviderDeactivated (reason='tenant_suspended') per config + HealthCheckDisabled (reason='tenant_suspended') per check.
hooks.note: In-flight alerts continue to firing → auto-resolve when signals clear. New alerts do NOT dispatch (the disabled health checks stop feeding the evaluator). This is intentional — a suspended tenant is not actively operating; we don't want to keep paging.

hooks.name: ResumeMonitoringProvidersOnTenantResumed
hooks.purpose: Reverses SuspendMonitoringProvidersOnTenantSuspended. Only re-activates providers + checks that were disabled BY the suspension cascade (checks the audit trail for reason='tenant_suspended'). Providers / checks the tenant admin manually deactivated stay off. Restarts RunHealthCheckJob scheduling for re-enabled health checks.

no_bootstrapper.reason: Monitoring does not need a tenant-boot hydration path — every operation is on-demand load from the DB. Contrast with modules like `tenancy` (bootstraps request-context) or `settings` (bootstraps config cache) that need per-request tenant hydration.
